title: potentilla pensylvanica var. missurica
intro: Potentilla pensylvanica var. missurica, also known as Missouri cinquefoil, is a perennial plant species belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to have wound-healing effects and to combat infections. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic agent, making it a valuable herb for treating various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are then used to treat various ailments.
